mnemonics.title: BFS QUEUE
mnemonics.content: BFS: QUEUE - QUICK UNDERTAKING EXPLORES LEVELS - Remember: Queue is used to explore levels in order.

mnemonics.title: DFS STACK
mnemonics.content: DFS: DEEP FIRST SEARCH - Goes deep before going wide, uses Stack (or recursion).

mnemonics.title: MATRIX SPACE
mnemonics.content: MATRIX: MOSTLY TAKES ROOM IN X-Y - Adjacency matrix is O(V²) - lots of space needed.

mnemonics.title: LIST SPACE
mnemonics.content: LIST: LITTLE SPACE - Adjacency list is O(V+E) - efficient for sparse graphs.

mnemonics.title: VISITED PREVENTS
mnemonics.content: VISITED: VITAL IN SEARCHING TRAVERSALS - Visited array prevents revisiting and infinite loops.

acronyms.usage: Level-order traversal using queue, finds shortest path in unweighted graphs
Depth-order traversal using stack/recursion, used for cycle detection, topological sort
Used to denote vertex count in graph complexity analysis
Used to denote edge count in graph complexity analysis
Graph ADT defines operations without implementation details

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a chessboard where rows and columns represent vertices. A piece at position (i,j) means an edge exists from i to j.
Think of a city where each vertex is a station, and each station has a list of direct train connections to other stations.
Imagine ripples spreading in a pond from a stone - each ring represents vertices at increasing distance from source.
Think of exploring a maze by always taking the rightmost available path until hitting a dead end, then backtracking.
Picture a circular road - you can drive around infinitely without reaching an end. Cycles in graphs are similar paths that return to starting point.

analogies.analogy: Adjacency matrix is like a complete friendship list (knowing everyone whether you talk to them or not), while adjacency list is like only remembering friends you actually contact.
BFS is like checking all people at your immediate distance before moving to the next circle - first friends, then friends of friends.
DFS is like exploring a cave by following one tunnel as far as possible before trying another branch.
Visited array is like marking paths you've already walked in a forest to avoid walking in circles.
Connected components are like separate islands - you can travel anywhere within an island but cannot reach another island without a bridge.
